summary: Fixed the decompilation match for fighter common down-bound routine ftCo_80097D40 by extracting the shared branch logic into a static helper inlineA1, using GET_FIGHTER consistently, and leaving ftCo_80097D40 responsible for the final downspot field reset. The PR was small, merged quickly, and had no substantive review feedback in the provided slice.
what_changed: In src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound.c, moved the branch on Fighter.x2228_b2 out of ftCo_80097D40 into a new static helper named inlineA1.
Changed the helper to fetch the Fighter pointer with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) instead of the prior direct gobj->user_data access in ftCo_80097D40.
ftCo_80097D40 now gets Fighter* fp via GET_FIGHTER(gobj), calls inlineA1(gobj), then sets fp->mv.co.downspot.x4 = 0.
Removed the prior local dummy padding array `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` from ftCo_80097D40.
Kept the existing `#define SOLUTION 1` / `#undef SOLUTION` wrapper around ftCo_80097D40, but moved it after the helper definition.
smart_moves: Separated the branch/action-selection logic from the field reset so the compiler could produce the target layout for ftCo_80097D40 without artificial stack padding.
Used a small static helper for code-shape control, a common decomp tactic when an apparent inline boundary affects matching.
Used GET_FIGHTER consistently in both the helper and ftCo_80097D40, aligning with project style and avoiding direct reliance on gobj->user_data in this routine.
Preserved behavior: the x2228_b2 branch still chooses between ftCo_80097AF4 and ftCo_8009794C, and ftCo_80097D40 still clears mv.co.downspot.x4 afterward.
decomp_lessons: When a function only fails to match because of ordering or stack-shape issues, extracting candidate inline logic into a static helper can be cleaner than keeping dummy locals or padding arrays.
Avoid fake stack-padding locals like `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` when an inline/helper boundary can explain the generated code more naturally.
For fighter code, prefer GET_FIGHTER(gobj) when retrieving Fighter* unless the evidence specifically requires direct `gobj->user_data` access.
A helper named `inlineA1` indicates the change is primarily a matching tactic, not a semantic API addition; future cleanup may rename only if stronger symbol evidence appears.
naming_conventions: Existing generated/unknown fighter function names remained in the ftCo_80097xxx style: ftCo_80097D40, ftCo_80097AF4, ftCo_8009794C, ftCo_80097D88.
The matching helper was named `inlineA1`, following a temporary/decomp-helper convention rather than a domain-semantic name.
The Fighter object accessor macro GET_FIGHTER(gobj) was used instead of direct `gobj->user_data` access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a static helper to influence inlining/codegen around ftCo_80097D40.
Removed an artificial 8-byte local array previously used as a matching workaround.
Kept `#define SOLUTION 1` around the target function, suggesting the file uses solution-gated matching alternatives or compiler-shape hints.
Factored the x2228_b2 conditional call sequence before the final `fp->mv.co.downspot.x4 = 0` store to match the expected instruction order/structure.
follow_up_queries: Search for other `u8 _[...]` dummy padding locals in fighter code that might be replaceable with helper extraction or inline-boundary modeling.
Check whether `inlineA1` remains a temporary helper or later PRs rename/remove it after improved symbol knowledge.
Compare other ftCo_DownBound routines using GET_FIGHTER versus direct gobj->user_data to identify consistent accessor style.
